metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Edge of Eternities is a fun but somewhat unbalanced set. Green is the dominant color, tempo matters enormously, mana fixing is scarce (Landers are the primary fixer), and the format rewards early board presence combined with card advantage.

color_notes.{G} Green: Best color. Deep common pool, superior creature sizing, best pump spell (Biosynthic Burst), best mana fixing access, and all its archetypes are playable.
color_notes.{B} Black: Deep and consistent. Two premium removal spells (Depressurize, Gravkill), strong early creatures, and cool sacrifice synergies. Some bad filler (Gravblade Heavy, Dark Endurance, Temporal Intervention).
color_notes.{U} Blue: Has the best common in the set (Cryogen Relic). Reasonably deep with only one truly awful card (Sinister Cryologist). Great with artifact synergies.
color_notes.{W} White: Polarizing color. Offers medium-sized creatures (Starfighter Pilot, Brightspear Zealot) and excellent spells (Knight Luminary, Banishing Light, Focus Fire, Auxiliary Boosters). Can go under bigger green decks.
color_notes.{R} Red: Weakest color. Some strong commons (Orbital Plunge, Zookeeper Mechan, Kav Landseeker) but lots of bad filler (Drill Too Deep, Red Tiger Mechan, Kavaron Skywarden, Frontline War-Rager). Part of two of the worst archetypes.

key_gameplay_notes: Tempo is king — early board presence is essential; games often snowball quickly.
Card advantage is also critical — staying gassed up is just as important as early plays.
Mana fixing is scarce; EOE has no common dual lands. 9/8 sources is typically the best you can do.
Landers are best used as part of two-spell turns rather than as standalone plays.
Several board wipes exist: Lithobraking (nonrare), Beyond the Quiet, Zero Point Ballad, Terminal Velocity, Mutinous Massacre, Singularity Rupture, Extinguisher Battleship.

mechanics.name: Spacecraft and Station
mechanics.summary: Spacecraft are artifact creatures that require tapping a creature (station) to put charge counters on them, granting abilities when fully charged. Best played in small numbers — drawing all spacecraft with no creatures is a disaster.
mechanics.performance: Not format-defining but meaningful. Value spacecraft overperform; clunky ones underperform.

mechanics.name: Warp
mechanics.summary: Warp lets you cast a card for an alternate cost (usually face-down or exile), offering flexibility and card advantage. Quality varies enormously from near-mythic (Codecracker Hound, Starfield Shepherd) to workhorse (Knight Luminary, Germinating Wurm) to bad filler (Sinister Cryologist, Red Tiger Mechan).
mechanics.performance: Solid all-around mechanic. Key enabler for Void.

mechanics.name: Void
mechanics.summary: A triggered bonus mechanic for black-based decks that activates when a permanent leaves the battlefield. Often inconvenient but usually achievable. Sequence carefully — pairing a removal spell with a void payoff on the same turn is ideal.
enablers: Use a removal spell on an opponent's creature
Sacrifice to Slagdrill Scrapper or Swarm Culler
Sacrifice self (Cryogen Relic, Illvoi Galeblade, Nutrient Block)

mechanics.name: Lander Tokens
mechanics.summary: Artifact land tokens that sacrifice to produce mana of any color. Primary source of mana fixing in EOE. Also enable void, trigger landfall, and turn on artifact synergies. Best used as part of a two-spell turn rather than alone.

mechanics.name: Planets
mechanics.summary: A mythic land cycle that slowly accumulates charge counters (needs 12+) to unlock a powerful reward. Low opportunity cost — essentially just a tapped basic. Can also interact with 'tapped matters' creatures like Flight-Deck Coordinator.

planets.name: Adagia, Windswept Bastion
planets.note: Strongest planet for Limited. Copies enchantments — great targets include Banishing Light, Cryogen Relic, Dubious Delicacy, Pinnacle Kill-Ship.

planets.name: Kavaron, Memorial World
planets.note: Generates unlimited Imodane's Recruiter tokens once active. Worth including in nearly every red deck.

planets.name: Susur Secundi, Void Altar
planets.note: Can be game-winning but requires careful setup. Best in BG with large creatures and cheap removal. Sorcery-speed activation is a serious limitation.

planets.name: Uthros, Titanic Godcore
planets.note: By the time it activates you have plenty of mana. Very low draft priority.

planets.name: Evendo, Waking Haven
planets.note: Mana payoff comes too late to matter. Only consider in specific ramp builds.

tier_1.name: Simic Ramp
tier_1.strategy: Combine green's ramp and fatties with blue's card advantage and artifact synergies. Splash freely with Landers and Gene Pollinator. Biomechan Engineer is the best signpost in the set. Build around getting ahead on board and card advantage simultaneously.

tier_2.name: Selesnya Counters
tier_2.strategy: Focus on +1/+1 counters using Haliya, Ascendant Cadet and Rayblade Trooper, or pivot into a GW midrange/ramp pile that ignores the counter theme. Flexible archetype with multiple build paths.

tier_2.name: Gruul Landfall
tier_2.strategy: Leverage Lander tokens and landfall payoffs alongside green's big creatures and red's direct damage. Kav Landseeker and Orbital Plunge are key early pieces. Strong synergy between green ramp and red landfall payoffs.

tier_2.name: Azorius Second Spell
tier_2.strategy: Build around casting two spells per turn to trigger 'second spell' payoffs. White's small efficient creatures plus blue's card draw and counter magic. Strong early clock backed by card advantage.

tier_3.name: Orzhov Go Wide
tier_3.strategy: Flood the board with small creatures and use go-wide payoffs. White's weenies combine with black's sacrifice synergies and void enablers. Perigee Beckoner is a key synergy piece.

tier_3.name: Dimir Artifacts
tier_3.strategy: Leverage artifact synergies with blue's Cryogen Relic and black's sacrifice outlets. Selfcraft Mechan and Exosuit Savior are key payoffs. Has access to powerful rare sweepers (Mutinous Massacre) as a bonus.

tier_3.name: Rakdos Void
tier_3.strategy: Enable void consistently through sacrifice, combat trading, and removal to power up Plasma Bolt and Decode Transmissions. Black provides premium removal and red adds direct damage. Sequence turns carefully to maximize void triggers.

tier_3.name: Izzet Artifacts
tier_3.strategy: Combine blue's artifact payoffs with red's direct damage and spacecraft. Lacks the depth of UB but can still go over the top with the right rares. One of the weaker archetypes overall.

tier_4.name: Golgari Graveyard / Midrange
tier_4.strategy: The 'graveyard' theme is underdeveloped. Best played as a midrange pile leveraging green fatties and black removal. Susur Secundi, Void Altar is a build-around if you can protect your life total. Requires specific rares to be competitive.

tier_4.name: Boros Spacecraft
tier_4.strategy: Intended to station spacecraft with white's small creatures and red's attackers, but the theme is underpowered. Spacecraft are risky — too many is a liability. Only worth pursuing with multiple premium spacecraft and strong supporting rares.

top_commons.name: Knight Luminary
top_commons.note: Workhorse warp card with strong base stats.

top_commons.name: Banishing Light
top_commons.note: Premium removal; top target for Adagia, Windswept Bastion.

top_commons.name: Auxiliary Boosters
top_commons.note: Source of inevitability in longer games.

top_uncommons.name: Haliya, Ascendant Cadet
top_uncommons.note: GW signpost; powers up the counters archetype.

top_uncommons.name: Dubious Delicacy
top_uncommons.note: Top enchantment target for Adagia copy effects.

top_uncommons.name: Pinnacle Kill-Ship
top_uncommons.note: Powerful spacecraft/removal hybrid; great Adagia target.

top_commons.name: Cryogen Relic
top_commons.note: Best common in the entire set. Sacrifice for value; Adagia copy target.

top_commons.name: Nanoform Sentinel
top_commons.note: Efficient artifact creature with useful ability.

top_commons.name: Selfcraft Mechan
top_commons.note: Strong synergy with Cryogen Relic and artifact payoffs.

top_commons.name: Temporal Intervention
top_commons.note: Flexible instant-speed interaction (note: often a stinker).

top_uncommons.name: Codecracker Hound
top_uncommons.note: Near-mythic warp card; 2-for-1 that creates board presence.

top_uncommons.name: Starfield Shepherd
top_uncommons.note: Excellent warp uncommon with powerful effect.

top_uncommons.name: Biomechan Engineer
top_uncommons.note: GU signpost; best signpost uncommon in the set.

top_uncommons.name: Genemorph Imago
top_uncommons.note: GU signpost; absurdly powerful when built around.

top_commons.name: Depressurize
top_commons.note: Premium removal spell; one of the best in the set.

top_commons.name: Gravkill
top_commons.note: Top-tier removal; pairs perfectly with void payoffs.

top_commons.name: Virus Beetle
top_commons.note: Strong 2-drop with discard; enables sacrifice lines.

top_uncommons.name: Swarm Culler
top_uncommons.note: Reliable void enabler and sacrifice outlet.

top_uncommons.name: Slagdrill Scrapper
top_uncommons.note: Top sacrifice outlet; enables void efficiently.

top_commons.name: Orbital Plunge
top_commons.note: One of red's best commons; efficient damage spell.

top_commons.name: Zookeeper Mechan
top_commons.note: Strong common with relevant creature type.

top_commons.name: Kav Landseeker
top_commons.note: Key landfall engine for RG archetype.

top_uncommons.name: Imodane's Recruiter
top_uncommons.note: Key payoff for Kavaron, Memorial World.

top_commons.name: Galactic Wayfarer
top_commons.note: Top green common; 2-for-1 that creates board presence AND a Lander.

top_commons.name: Gene Pollinator
top_commons.note: Second-best green common; enables all splashes effectively.

top_commons.name: Biosynthic Burst
top_commons.note: Best common pump spell in the format; tempo blowout.

top_commons.name: Diplomatic Relations
top_commons.note: Green removal; removal that competes with other colors' offerings.

top_commons.name: Meltstrider's Resolve
top_commons.note: Conditional fight effect; solid green removal.

top_uncommons.name: Fungal Colossus
top_uncommons.note: Massive landfall/ramp payoff for multi-color builds.

top_uncommons.name: Starwinder
top_uncommons.note: Powerful green uncommon with ramp upside.

top_uncommons.name: Rayblade Trooper
top_uncommons.note: GW counters signpost with strong base body.

top_commons.name: Nutrient Block
top_commons.note: Sacrifice for void; life gain artifact.

notable_rares.name: Weftwalking
notable_rares.note: Powerful effect; bold inclusion but high ceiling in the right deck.

notable_rares.name: Mutinous Massacre
notable_rares.note: Near-game-winning sweeper for Rakdos. Highest priority if not in those colors.

notable_rares.name: Singularity Rupture
notable_rares.note: Nondiscriminating sweeper; splash target in green decks.

draft_plan.pack_1: Stay open. Prioritize premium removal and cards that play well in multiple archetypes. Green cards are likely to wheel less than other colors — pick them when they're clearly the best card. Avoid early commitment to spacecraft-heavy builds.
draft_plan.pack_2: Solidify your colors. Draft around your signpost uncommon if you have one. Prioritize cards that thread the needle on tempo and card advantage. Begin identifying splash options if you have enough Lander generators.
draft_plan.pack_3: Fill out your curve. Look for 2-drops and 3-drops to back up your top-end. Secure additional removal. Consider adding a splash target only if you have 2+ cheap Lander generators and a relevant basic.

general_tips: Prefer cards that create both board presence AND card advantage (e.g., Galactic Wayfarer, Codecracker Hound).
Be green or have a plan to beat green — its creature sizing dominates boards.
Don't mulligan one-basic hands; mana fixing is too scarce to risk it.
Landers are best cracked as part of a two-spell turn, not wasted on mana alone.
Play 1-2 Spacecraft max unless your deck is purpose-built around them.
Void is often inconvenient — sequence your turns carefully to enable it efficiently.
Be aware of board wipes; avoid overextending against Rakdos and Dimir opponents in particular.
